text: Nice to see Sam getting interviewed by an actual AI.

text: Sam seems like a human person. I like that he asks Lex questions right back and waits curiously for his answer.

text: I like that Sam also asks Lex questions. Shows true engagement and curiosity!

text: Lex podcasts are a dream come true, never thought in my life i could listen to tech people (Creator of  Java, Python, C++ etc) speak their mind on a podcast. Lex thank you bro.  Salute to your passion.
author: @thanosbaba1

text: Two AI guys discussing AI in a way we can easily understand it is priceless. More of this please and thanks for sharing 👍
author: @TheRealStructurer

text: "We want to make mistakes while the stakes are low". Much respect for Sam Altman for treading with such care.
author: @patrickkranzpiller6400

text: I didn't think we'd get close to AGI in my lifetime (I'm 56), but having used GPT-4, in both work (as a software engineer) and personally, I feel I might actually live to see it. We live in very exciting and discombobulating times.
author: @BeatPoet67

text: Thank you again gents for breaking down the silos and sharing through clarity and explanation.
author: @CanadianPodcast

text: Amazing. ❤
author: @alexbarn

text: @tsoupakis  Number of characters is irrelevant. Possibly number of tokens and their type (which would need to be weighted). His point was who stupid it is to define the value of a model based on such criteria. Like almost all arguments for this psycholog, he reframe a complex problem in 6 "facts" and then try to convinced you because the other five are wrong, the sixth, his argument is right. His ideas in general are the dust under the carpet and not the essence of a concept.
author: @AlexDubois

text: Did you not even ask him about his doomsday compound? That's the most interesting thing about the man who's causing the apocalypse.
author: @chrisheist652

text: In 5 years this will be the most hated man in the planet
author: @kh9242

text: It sucks, let’s start with that
author: @jerry8405

text:
I really hate people who shout 'ethics'.
We are running out of time and life span.
Our parents die painfully of cancer in the near future, or their bodies are broken and completely incapacitated.
Therefore, do not do useless things, our ultimate goal is reverse aging and life extension.
The longer the lifespan, the longer the research can last.
This world is already dystopian enough, a hellish planet, to even question it.
author: @crunchcoral4859

text: It's so hard to listen to. Which will destroy society first?
author: @georgevaccaro9379

text: I thought the opposite actually. I felt he was manipulating Lex and the audience. Also the time he took for every answer felt like a pose to me.
author: @cellowify

text: 23:03 quite nasty and kind of dirty form Mr Altman, to present as a problem the number of characters and NOT the barriers they implemented that had as a result a difference in characters numbers.. The problem was never about having more characters in one political party, the problem is the refusal to discuss not political correct, left leaning, pg 12 ideas. The problem is that the AI is worried more about not make you a "nazi" instead of worried on provide you with a truth answer..
author: @tsoupakis

text: Things Lex absolutely believes are true: ~1h50m. Why not mention oh, Idk, "I know my mother loves me" ? No he goes with math and historical battle dates.
author: @nibnob9

text:
A fun interview, but the vocal fry affectation is painful. What on Earth has happened in the US culture that people feel the need to affect this voice; do they feel the need for gravitas, but miss and end up with just gravel? : )
"the artificial thought guy has an artificial voice" ?
